title: ophrys aranifera subsp. helenae
intro: Ophrys aranifera subsp. helenae, also known as the Spider Orchid, is a rare and endemic plant species found in Greece. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Ophrys aranifera subsp. helenae is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its use is still largely anecdotal and requires further scientific research for validation.
